prompt:
Runtime guardrails:
- You have approximately 900 seconds total.
- When waiting for GitHub checks or AI review, run `gh pr checks` every 30 seconds instead of using long fixed sleeps.
- Any single `sleep` longer than 30 seconds will be capped by the runner shell.
- Prioritize: fix -> targeted validation -> push -> poll checks.
- IMPORTANT: you are running in a non-interactive environment with no terminal. Do NOT run `git rebase --edit-todo` or any command that opens an editor (nano, vim). GIT_SEQUENCE_EDITOR=true and GIT_EDITOR=true are set in the runner environment so these commands will silently succeed without an editor.

Goal: make PR #12 mergeable without merging it.

Requirements:
1. Work only inside the existing PR branch.
   - branch: codex/default-timeout-10m
   - worktree: /home/svtter/work/project/opencode-actions/.worktrees/default-timeout-10m
   - pull request: https://github.com/Svtter/opencode-actions/pull/12
2. Inspect the current blockers with GitHub CLI before changing code:
   - gh pr view 12 --json mergeable,mergeStateStatus,reviewDecision,statusCheckRollup
   - gh pr checks 12
   - gh pr view 12 --comments
3. Fix the underlying issues, commit to the same branch, and push to the PR branch.
4. After each fix, run the relevant local validation. Use the repo conventions from AGENTS.md and avoid destructive git commands.
5. After pushing, capture a final status snapshot and exit so the scheduler can take over external waiting:
   - run `gh pr checks 12` once to capture the current check state
   - then re-run `gh pr view 12 --json mergeable,mergeStateStatus,reviewDecision,statusCheckRollup`
   - if new review comments or failing checks appear, summarize them in your final output instead of waiting for them to settle
6. Do not merge the PR.

Comment-driven repair contract:
- The scheduler selected this PR because actionable review feedback still needs resolution.
- Do not stop just because the PR is already mergeable or checks are green.
- Success requires one of:
  - resolve the actionable review feedback with a commit pushed to this PR branch, or
  - if you verify the feedback is already satisfied at the current HEAD and no code change is needed, write `.ralph/runner-result.json` with JSON exactly shaped like:
    `{"status": "already_satisfied", "summary": "<brief evidence-based explanation>"}`
- Only use the `already_satisfied` path after checking the cited feedback against the current code.


Constraints:
- Do not develop on main.
- Respect any existing user changes in the worktree.
- Prefer minimal, targeted fixes.
- Work within the runner time budget exposed at execution time and avoid long `sleep` commands.
- Use the repository's existing style and validation commands.
- When code changes are made, run a local review pass and address material findings before finishing.
- Follow AGENTS.md instructions if the repository has that file.
